title: Per capita electricity generation from fossil fuels
caption:
Per capita electricity generation from fossil fuels

Electricity generation from coal, oil, and gas, measured in kilowatt-hours per person.

This choropleth world map displays 2025 per capita electricity generation from fossil fuels, measured in kilowatt-hours per person. The legend spans from 0 kWh to over 10,000 kWh, with specific tick points at 100, 200, 500, 1,000, 2,000, and 5,000. The darkest shades, indicating values above 5,000 kWh, are visible in the United States, Canada, Russia, Australia, and several countries in the Middle East including Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates. These regions sit in the highest visible bands of the color scale. Surrounding these areas, countries in Western Europe, China, and parts of South America appear in mid-range orange shades that fall between the 1,000 and 5,000 kWh thresholds. Much of Africa and South Asia shows lighter shades, with many countries falling below the 500 kWh mark. India and Indonesia sit in a slightly darker band than their immediate neighbors but remain in the lower-to-mid range of the legend. Greenland and a few small island territories show hatching, indicating no data is available. The visual pattern shows the highest per capita generation concentrated in North America, parts of the Middle East, and Australia, while the lowest values are distributed across much of Africa and parts of Asia.
